articles.title: OA2500 - NHA Mock Exam Grades
articles.description: Accessing Grades in NHA. Steps for logging in and accessing learner grades and progress in the NHA platform. Log in. From the Instructor Dashboard, select Learning Insights Center. Select Performanceâ¦
articles.short_version: Accessing Grades in NHA. Steps for logging in and accessing learner grades and progress in the NHA platform. Log in. From the Instructor Dashboard, select Learning Insights Center. Select Performanceâ¦
articles.body:
Accessing Grades in NHA

Steps for logging in and accessing learner grades and progress in the NHA platform.

  1. Log in
  2. From the Instructor Dashboard, select Learning Insights Center
  1. Select Performance Analytics to see learner specific information for the course
  1. Choose the following content from the drop-down menu:
    1. Nasium Training, LLC
    2. Select course (scroll down until you see the live sections and look for the start date; hover your mouse cursor over the course to see the information appear)
    3. Select APPLY
  2. After the page refreshes, you'll see the course overview snapshot. A high level overview of all students will appear in the 3 boxes: In the past week; Module Progress, and Module Performance - Across Products
    1. Across Products = the different NHA content that has been placed in the course such as CCMA Study Guide, PersonAbility, etc.
    2. Select NEXT in that 3rd box (Module Performance - Across Products) for more detailed information

  1. Scroll down the page to find specific learners as well as the various tabs to view learner-specific work and completion data:

Finding Learner Grades

  1. Choose learner from the list; click on their blue hyperlink.
  2. Click on the ASSESSMENTS tab to access point values for each grade.
NOTE: Modules are not graded items. Clicking on the MODULES tab will show you whether a learner has accessed the content and completed the content.

  1. Scroll through the Assessments to find the point value.
    1. Below each score overview, you can find specific topics the learner may want to review before taking the next practice exam. Topics are recommended based on missed items on the exam.
  2. Back in the D2L gradebook, input the highest grade the learner received (found by scrolling through the learner info under the MODULES tab in the NHA platform).
    1. Be sure to add the points and NOT the percent.

  1. Do the same for the PersonAbility ESSENTIAL SKILLS and the PersonAbility FINAL ASSESSMENT grades.
    1. They are located in the same MODULES tab on the NHA platform where you found the CCMA Practice Exam scores.

articles.title: Adding Smarter Measure FCA_All and FCA_Five External Learning Tools
articles.description: Purpose. The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to add Smarter Measure FCA_All and FCA_Five External Learning Tools Procedure. Go to Required Skills Assessment section in course work. Go to Add Exâ¦
articles.short_version: Purpose. The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to add Smarter Measure FCA_All and FCA_Five External Learning Tools Procedure. Go to Required Skills Assessment section in course work. Go to Add Exâ¦
articles.body:
Purpose

The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to add Smarter Measure FCA_All and FCA_Five External Learning Tools

Procedure

  1. Go to Required Skills Assessment section in course work

  1. Go to Add Existing

  1. Click on External Tool Activity

  1. Search for FCA

  1. Click on FCA ALL SmarterMeasure or FCA Five SmarterMeasure based on the course listing below

ME1000-FCA_All

MA1015P-FCA_Five

HT1300-FCA_All

MA1015C-FCA_All

MG2150-FCA_All

ME1200-FCA_Five

HT1000-FCA_All

Â 

  1. Click Continue in new window to confirm link opens

you should see this

  1. Unhide the link

  1. Click on Edit

  1. Rename link to

Smarter Measure - Readiness

  1. Make points out of 100

  1. Click on Edit or Link to Existing

  1. Choose Create and link to a new grade item then click on OK

  1. Select Save and Close

  2. Go to Grades

  1. Go to Grades

  1. Go to Manage Grades

  1. Select the Chevron next to the grade column

  1. Click on Edit

  1. Click on Edit

  1. Select Exclude from Final Grade Calculation

  1. Change Grade Scheme to Complete/Incomplete

  1. Select Save and Close

  1. Go to more actions and Reorder

  1. Go to more actions and Reorder

  1. Go to more actions and Reorder

  1. Move it so it is after Week 5

  1. Test using a student account

  1. You should see this

  1. Date manage the item back in the course

Start 1/28/2022 12:00 PM

End Date 2/20/2022 11:59 PM

articles.title: Set up Evolve Activities in D2L
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to link Evolve activities in a D2L course. 1) Go to Existing Activities 2) Select External Learning Tools 3) Select Evolve Link 4) The Evolve link is in the coursâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to link Evolve activities in a D2L course. 1) Go to Existing Activities 2) Select External Learning Tools 3) Select Evolve Link 4) The Evolve link is in the coursâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to link Evolve activities in a D2L course.

1) Go to Existing Activities

2) Select External Learning Tools

3) Select Evolve Link

4) The Evolve link is in the course

5) This is the link to help with linking

Institution-Hosted LMS Using the Evolve Link Tool - Elsevier Education

6) Click on the textbook link

7) Choose what you want to link

8) In this example Flashcards will be linked

9)

10) Go to Course Admin and External Learning Tools

11) Go to Manage Tool Links (Legacy)

12) Create New Link

13) Paste in url and description and select radio buttons

14) Select Save and Close

15) Go back to Coursework and select External Learning Tools

16) Select Activity

16) Activity is not deployed, test using a student account

17) Go back to Coursework and select External Learning Tools

18) Verify that the link works

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

11/07/2025

Kevan Van Cleave

Kevan Van Cleave

articles.title: ITS - Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, create Excel file, then Word file in ChatGPTÂ - Not Respondus
articles.description: PURPOSE. The purpose of this job aid is to document the process for preparing an assignment or assessment in the LMS test tool using Respondus formatting. PROCEDURE. Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, createâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSE. The purpose of this job aid is to document the process for preparing an assignment or assessment in the LMS test tool using Respondus formatting. PROCEDURE. Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, createâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSE

The purpose of this job aid is to document the process for preparing an assignment or assessment in the LMS test tool using Respondus formatting.

PROCEDURE

Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, create Excel file, then Word file in ChatGPT

Step A: Extract Quiz Pool from D2L.

Brightspace Native Export (Brightspace Package / XML)Â 

You can use Brightspaceâs built-inÂ Import/Export/Copy ComponentsÂ tool to export quiz/question content.Â 

Â 

  1. Go toÂ Course Admin â Import/Export/Copy Components.Â 

Â Â 

  1. ChooseÂ Export as Brightspace Package.Â Then click Start.

Â 

  1. SelectÂ Question LibraryÂ (or Quizzes) and select "Select individual items to export". Then click continue.

Â Â 

  1. Check the box of the assessment you want to extract.Â ClickÂ Continue.Â 

Â Â 

  1. Do not check the box. ClickÂ Export.Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. Export started. Click on Done.
    You may have to wait until you see aÂ notification in the "bell"Â icon above.Â (SeeÂ nextÂ 2 screenshots.)Â Â 

You may have to wait a few minutesÂ before the red dot appearsÂ byÂ the bell icon.Â Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. Click on the bell icon.Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. ClickÂ on the blue link with the assessment name you are extracting.Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. Download the resulting ZIP file by clicking on the blue link.Â Â 

Â 

  1. It willÂ downloadÂ as a ZIP file. Keep itÂ asÂ is.Â Â 

This gives youÂ IMS QTI/XML filesÂ containingÂ questions that other tools or converters can read.Â Â 

Â 

Step B: Ask ChatGPT extract the file into an Excel file.Â 

  1. Go to ChatGPT.Â 
  2. Start a newÂ chat in ChatGPT and attach your Zip fileÂ with the + (plus) sign on the ChatGPT text bubble.Â 

Â Â Â Â Â Â (disregardÂ the text in the bubble above.)Â Â 

  1. Use this prompt (all of these sentences):Â Â 

IÂ have exported a zip file usingÂ Brightspace Native Export (Brightspace Package / XML).Â Â 

PleaseÂ unnzipÂ Brightspace export packageÂ andÂ convert this file to text in an MS Excel document.Â 

Target theÂ QTI assessmentÂ Item structureÂ that D2L usesÂ and create anÂ Excel file.Â 

CreateÂ theÂ ExcelÂ fileÂ using the D2L/QTI 1.x structureÂ ifÂ necessaryÂ so the content appears in the Excel file.Â 

PreserveÂ all original wording.Â 

Â 

  1. After waiting a few minutes, you should get an Excel file that looks like this, depending on how many questions itÂ contains.
    Download it and adjust the columns.Â 

Â 

  1. After the first one, you can use this prompt and attach the next zipped assessment file.Â 

Â (Note that it may not download matching question text correctly.)

  1. Download the file.
  2. You can rename the file to an appropriate course name like SC1000_W2L3_Assessment_Pool_1 .

Step C: Convert Excel sheet to MS Word file

1. Create a new chat in ChatGPT.

2. Copy this prompt (all of these sentences) into ChatGPT. -- Also: Attach the Excel file.

I exported a question bank from D2L Brightspace from the question library and then had ChatGPT convert it to an Excel spreadsheet file.

Can you convert this Excel spreadsheet file into an MS Word file (with the questions, answers, and correct answers indicated) so it is formatted so it can be added to Respondus test question generating software, with proper formatting for Respondus.

Do not change any wording in the file.

Please ignore any extra information in this Excel file that is not needed to generate the questions and answers with correct answers noted.

3. After using the prompt once, you can prompt, "Do the same thing with this file." with the next file you want to convert to an MS Word file.

4. Rename the file to the correct name.

5. Check that the questions match the course questions.

  1. Go to ChatGPT.Â 
  2. Start a newÂ chat in ChatGPT and attach your Zip fileÂ with the + (plus) sign on the ChatGPT text bubble.Â 

Â Â Â Â Â Â (disregardÂ the text in the bubble above.)Â Â 

  1. Use this prompt (all of these sentences):Â Â 

IÂ have exported a zip file usingÂ Brightspace Native Export (Brightspace Package / XML).Â Â 

PleaseÂ unnzipÂ Brightspace export packageÂ andÂ convert this file to text in an MS Excel document.Â 

Target theÂ QTI assessmentÂ Item structureÂ that D2L usesÂ and create anÂ Excel file.Â 

CreateÂ theÂ ExcelÂ fileÂ using the D2L/QTI 1.x structureÂ ifÂ necessaryÂ so the content appears in the Excel file.Â 

PreserveÂ all original wording.Â 

Â 

  1. After waiting a few minutes, you should get an Excel file that looks like this, depending on how many questions itÂ contains.
    Download it and adjust the columns.Â 

Â 

  1. After the first one, you can use this prompt and attach the next zipped assessment file.Â 

Â (Note that it may not download matching question text correctly.)

  1. Download the file.
  2. You can rename the file to an appropriate course name like SC1000_W2L3_Assessment_Pool_1 .

Step C: Convert Excel sheet to MS Word file

1. Create a new chat in ChatGPT.

2. Copy this prompt (all of these sentences) into ChatGPT. -- Also: Attach the Excel file.

I exported a question bank from D2L Brightspace from the question library and then had ChatGPT convert it to an Excel spreadsheet file.

Can you convert this Excel spreadsheet file into an MS Word file (with the questions, answers, and correct answers indicated) so it is formatted so it can be added to Respondus test question generating software, with proper formatting for Respondus.

Do not change any wording in the file.

Please ignore any extra information in this Excel file that is not needed to generate the questions and answers with correct answers noted.

3. After using the prompt once, you can prompt, "Do the same thing with this file." with the next file you want to convert to an MS Word file.

4. Rename the file to the correct name.

5. Check that the questions match the course questions.

articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to link Evolve activities in a D2L course. 1) Go to Existing Activities 2) Select External Learning Tools 3) Select Evolve Link 4) The Evolve link is in the coursâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to link Evolve activities in a D2L course.

1) Go to Existing Activities

2) Select External Learning Tools

3) Select Evolve Link

4) The Evolve link is in the course

5) Click on the link

6) Then copy the url from the desired Sherpath
